2606.13209.title: Understanding helpfulness and harmless tension in reward models
2606.13209.paper_summary: Reward models are a key component of reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), aligning language models toward both helpful and harmless behaviour. However, the internal mechanisms underlying these objectives and their conflicts remain poorly understood. We study alignment tension in reward models trained under helpfulness-only, harmlessness-only, and mixed-objective settings. We find that mixed-objective models often underperform single-objective models, indicating interference between objectives. Using activation-based methods, we identify neurons associated with each objective and study their functional roles via targeted ablations. We find that these neurons causally support their corresponding objectives while often negatively affecting the opposing one. We find that a substantial proportion of neurons are shared between helpfulness and harmlessness, and that these shared neurons exert a disproportionate influence on model behaviour, contributing to alignment tension. Additionally, our results provide insights and mechanistic interpretation into how alignment objectives are represented in reward models and why multi-objective alignment remains challenging, motivating future work on disentangled and controllable alignment methods.
2606.13209.paper_summary_zh: 獎勵模型是來自人類反饋的強化學習（RLHF）的關鍵組成部分，旨在使語言模型朝向有幫助和無害的行為對齊。<lb/>然而，這些目標及其衝突背後的內部機制仍然不甚了解。<lb/>我們研究在僅有幫助性、僅有無害性和混合目標設置下訓練的獎勵模型中的對齊緊張。<lb/>我們發現混合目標模型通常表現不如單一目標模型，這表明目標之間存在干擾。<lb/>使用基於激活的方法，我們識別與每個目標相關的神經元，並通過有針對性的切除研究它們的功能角色。<lb/>我們發現這些神經元在因果上支持其相應的目標，同時經常對對立的目標產生負面影響。<lb/>我們發現有相當比例的神經元在有幫助性和無害性之間共享，這些共享的神經元對模型行為施加了不成比例的影響，進而導致對齊緊張。<lb/>此外，我們的結果提供了對獎勵模型中對齊目標如何被表示以及為何多目標對齊仍然具有挑戰性的見解和機制解釋，激勵未來在解耦和可控的對齊方法上的研究。
2606.13209.author: Eshaan Tanwar et.al.
